description: A continuous drug-discovery research service. Give it a disease or target profile, screening criteria, optional candidate seeds, literature notes, and an input folder; it continuously generates candidate hypotheses, screens them with DrugCLIP, folds targets, runs configured simulations across cluster workers, and writes review-only cycle reports until manually stopped.

intent.description: A continuous drug-discovery research service. Give it a disease or target profile, screening criteria, optional candidate seeds, literature notes, and an input folder; it continuously generates candidate hypotheses, screens them with DrugCLIP, folds targets, runs configured simulations across cluster workers, and writes review-only cycle reports until manually stopped.
intent.problem: Discovery work needs continuous, traceable candidate generation and scientific screening without collapsing cluster-scale folding and simulation into a single serial worker.
intent.target_users: Computational biology researchers, scientific AI platform teams, and research operations groups.
intent.customizable_for: Drug discovery, material search, assay planning, scientific literature review, and research pipeline support.

safety.reason: Human approval is required before high-impact downstream actions such as advance_to_lab_experiment, make_clinical_claim, submit_regulatory_material.

metadata.agent_role: AI service coordinator for distributed candidate generation, DrugCLIP virtual screening, folding, simulation, and evidence review.
metadata.customizable_for: Drug discovery, material search, assay planning, scientific literature review, and research pipeline support.
metadata.description: A continuous drug-discovery research service. Give it a disease or target profile, screening criteria, optional candidate seeds, literature notes, and an input folder; it continuously generates candidate hypotheses, screens them with DrugCLIP, folds targets, runs configured simulations across cluster workers, and writes review-only cycle reports until manually stopped.

human_control.reason: Human approval is required before high-impact downstream actions such as advance_to_lab_experiment, make_clinical_claim, submit_regulatory_material.

fields.description: Confirm the disease, phenotype, or target family to investigate.
fields.label: Disease or target

fields.description: Maximum runtime for the staged discovery loop.
fields.label: Runtime limit
Input folder
fields.description: Default local input folder for sample files or user-provided files.

fields.label: Output folder
fields.description: Default local output folder for generated artifacts and reports.

init_config_review.instruction: When running in OtterDesk, show these overwriteable fields during init config and ask the user to change them or keep the defaults before launch.

input_contract.description: Discovery work is iterative and evidence-heavy; researchers need an assistant that keeps candidate generation, scoring, extraction, and review moving in order.

optional_inputs.description: Research papers, notes, or database exports used as evidence.
optional_inputs.name: literature_corpus

optional_inputs.description: Wet-lab, ADMET, or computational constraints that shape recommendations.
optional_inputs.name: assay_constraints
input_folder
optional_inputs.description: Default local input folder for sample files or user-provided files.

optional_inputs.name: output_folder
optional_inputs.description: Default local output folder for generated artifacts and reports.

connector.description: Connector-provided target profile and evidence summaries from approved research systems.

file.description: JSON object loaded from a local file path.

json.description: Inline JSON object supplied through config or runtime overrides.

mock.description: Bundled Alzheimer demo target and synthetic candidate workflow.

sample_file.description: JSON disease profile, candidate seeds, and screening criteria loaded from a local file.

required_inputs.description: Disease, target, pathway, or phenotype that frames the discovery run.
required_inputs.name: disease_or_target_profile

required_inputs.description: Desired candidate, assay, safety, novelty, or evidence criteria.
required_inputs.name: screening_criteria

required_inputs.description: Optional seed molecules, protein targets, or prior candidates to compare.
required_inputs.name: candidate_seed_set

artifacts.description: Stable run-store files and domain-specific outputs.

human.description: Human notices, approval requests, responses, timeouts, and applied decisions.

input_skills.description: Connector-backed or reusable skill-backed input sources.

inputs.description: Resolved runtime inputs and adapter provenance.

logs.description: Runtime stdout/stderr and explicit log artifacts.

output_skills.description: Optional fan-out destinations after local run-store writes.

outputs.description: Primary result and final user-facing artifact.

resources.description: Resource samples such as token, runtime, connector, and disk usage.

streams.description: Named data or event streams exposed by the blueprint.

knowledge_rag.description: Optional local blueprint knowledge retrieval used to ground actor prompts and tool planning.

agentic_research.description: Bounded LLM-selected or actor-guided tool contract for domain work.

metadata.llm_agent_role: AI assistant for organizing drug discovery candidate generation, filtering, and evidence review.
metadata.name: Drug Discovery Research Assistant
metadata.output: Candidate artifacts, stage logs, evidence notes, and discovery summaries for researcher review.

final_artifact.description: The durable user-facing artifact should summarize the result, recommendation, confidence, evidence, next steps, and source references.

output_contract.stdout: single JSON object, event envelope, or bundle path

metadata.problem_solved: Discovery work needs continuous, traceable candidate generation and scientific screening without collapsing cluster-scale folding and simulation into a single serial worker.

quick_test.description: Use generated quick-test bundles when available; fixed blueprints use local dry-run/mock provider environment variables and deterministic sample inputs.

runtime_features: actor-style LLM specialists use retrieved policy context when available
metadata.simulation_type: Continuous distributed computational discovery service with DrugCLIP virtual screening.

standard.execution_model: load_metadata -> resolve_config -> start_native_service -> dispatch_cross_box_jobs -> generate_candidates -> fold_targets -> screen_with_drugclip -> simulate_selected_candidates -> aggregate_cycle_artifacts -> stop_on_manual_signal

metadata.target_user: Computational biology researchers, scientific AI platform teams, and research operations groups.
metadata.target_users: Computational biology researchers, scientific AI platform teams, and research operations groups.

knowledge_rag.purpose: Retrieve local discovery-triage guidance for target evidence, generated candidate review, docking caveats, and report-only scientific risk checks.
knowledge_rag.grounding_policy: Use retrieved local blueprint knowledge to improve actor review prompts; do not invent facts when retrieval is unavailable.
knowledge_rag.privacy: index local blueprint knowledge only; do not index private run documents unless a future runtime explicitly opts in

agentic_research.note: Scientific workers may use bounded tool outputs and local RAG guidance; failed or thin evidence remains explicit in the review packet.

service.manual_close: send SIGTERM or create the configured STOP file

collaboration.aggregation: fan in cycle artifacts into review-only reports

cluster_distribution.dispatch_contract: A configured dispatcher receives JSON jobs on stdin and returns JSON results or writes the declared output_path.

human.reason: Human approval is required before high-impact downstream actions such as advance_to_lab_experiment, make_clinical_claim, submit_regulatory_material.

steps.label: Target Discovery
steps.goal: Identify and summarize disease targets and biological constraints.

steps.label: Structure Generation
steps.goal: Generate or retrieve useful target structures for screening.

steps.label: Continuous Discovery Service
steps.goal: Continuously generate candidates, dispatch parallel folding, DrugCLIP screening, and simulation jobs until manually stopped.

steps.label: Cycle Results Review
steps.goal: Read the final continuous-service cycle summaries and preserve review-only scientific evidence.

steps.label: Ranking And Reporting
steps.goal: Rank candidates, summarize evidence, and prepare a human-reviewable research artifact.

screening_criteria.description: Desired candidate, assay, safety, novelty, or evidence criteria.

input_folder.description: Default local input folder for sample files or user-provided files.

output_folder.description: Default local output folder for generated artifacts and reports.

contracts.summary: A manually stopped native discovery service that continuously fans candidate, folding, DrugCLIP, and simulation work across configured cluster pools.
users: Computational biology researchers, scientific AI platform teams, and research operations groups.
